title: daucus pumilus
intro: Daucus pumilus, also known as the dwarf carrot or small wild carrot, is a biennial plant in the parsley family (Apiaceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Daucus pumilus include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and alleviate menstrual cramps. Daucus pumilus can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal remedies for its purported medicinal effects.
